den: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Slavey. (macrolanguage)
dent: noun. A shallow deformation in the surface of an object, produced by an impact.
due: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Umiray Dumaget Agta.
duet: noun. A musical composition in two parts, each performed by a single voice (singer, instrument or univoce ensemble).
dug: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chiduruma.
dun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dusun Deyah.
dune: noun. A ridge or hill of sand piled up by currents of wind or water.
dunt: noun. A stroke; a dull-sounding blow.
eng: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for English.
ged: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gade.
gem: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Germanic languages.
gen: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Geman Deng.
geum: noun. Any of the genus Geum of perennial herbaceous plants.
gnu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gnau.
gum: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guambiano.
gun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mbya Guarani.
gut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maléku Jaíka.
jet: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Manem.
jeu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Jonkor Bourmataguil.
med: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Melpa.
men: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (Sierra Leone).
mend: verb. To physically repair (something that is broken, defaced, decayed, torn, or otherwise damaged).
menu: noun. The details of the food to be served at a banquet; a bill of fare.
met: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mato.
mud: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mednyj Aleut.
mug: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Musgu.
mun: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Munda languages.
mut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Muria.
mute: adjective. Not having the power of speech; dumb.
muted: adjective. Not expressed strongly or openly; subdued and understated
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nude: adjective. Without clothing or other covering of the skin; without clothing on the genitals or female nipples.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
nutmeg: noun. An evergreen tree (Myristica fragrans) cultivated in the East Indies for its spicy seeds.
ted: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tepo Krumen.
teg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Teke-Tege.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tend: verb. To make a tender of; to offer or tender.
